preamble: The shift from the voiceless postalveolar fricative [ʃ] (sh) to the voiced postalveolar fricative [ʒ] (zh) is a standard form of voicing lenition. It typically occurs in intervocalic positions where the voiceless fricative is influenced by the surrounding voiced sounds.

examples.note: Individual or dialectal variation where the intervocalic sibilant is voiced.
External sandhi voicing where coda /s/ realized as [ʃ] becomes [ʒ] before a voiced consonant.

sources: Wells, J. C. (1982). Accents of English.
Ladefoged, P. (2001). A Course in Phonetics.
